     The Numbers Business Report

Ferrari (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $18,550,030Details
International Box Office $22,439,630
Worldwide Box Office $40,989,660
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $352,622
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $449,908
Total $802,530
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It is the summer of 1957. Behind the spectacle of Formula 1, ex-racer Enzo Ferrari is in a crisis. Bankruptcy threatens the factory he and his wife, Laura built from nothing ten years earlier. Their volatile marriage has been battered by the loss of their son, Dino a year earlier. Ferrari struggles to acknowledge his son Piero with Lina Lardi. Meanwhile, his drivers’ passion to win pushes them to the edge as they launch into the treacherous 1,000-mile race across Italy, the Mille Miglia.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$3,921,773 (21.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.73 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:45.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$95,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,386 opening theaters/2,386 max. theaters, 2.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $20,001,617

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2023 (Wide) by Neon
International Releases: December 14th, 2023 (Wide) (Italy)
December 21st, 2023 (Wide), released as Ferrari (Феррари) (Russia (CIS))
December 22nd, 2023 (Limited) (Turkey)
December 26th, 2023 (Limited) (United Kingdom)
December 26th, 2023 (Wide) (Poland)
Video Release: January 23rd, 2024 by Neon
MPA Rating: R for language, some sexual references and drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2806 (cert #54571), 10/25/2023)
Running Time: 124 minutes
Geographical setting: Italy
Plot point: Auto Racing, Death of a Son or Daughter, Money Troubles, Relationships Gone Wrong
Subgenre: Sports Drama
Time period setting: 1950s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Moto Pictures, Neon Films, STX Films, Forward Pass, Storyteller Media, Ketchup Entertainment, Esme Grace, Cecchi Gori USA, Red Sea Fund, Lady Bacardi Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

It is the summer of 1957. Behind the spectacle of Formula 1, ex-racer Enzo Ferrari is in a crisis. Bankruptcy threatens the factory he and his wife, Laura built from nothing ten years earlier. Their volatile marriage has been battered by the loss of their son, Dino a year earlier. Ferrari struggles to acknowledge his son Piero with Lina Lardi. Meanwhile, his drivers’ passion to win pushes them to the edge as they launch into the treacherous 1,000-mile race across Italy, the Mille Miglia.
